Details:
Bank filtration was effective in the removal of acetochlor, resulting in eliminating between 72 and 95 percent of the contaminant. It should be noted that the mean residence time for the bank filtration process was estimated at 6 days, suggesting other processes could have contributed to the herbicide removal (e.g. biological treatment).

Anodic Fenton Treatment, a process not typically seen in water treatment applications, was found to be effective in removing acetochlor. A study showed almost 98 percent removal in three minutes using a glass reactor consisting of two half-cells connected by an anion membrane.
March 2009
